steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: The left side is an independent clause with a prepositional phrase requiring an object (“rate of ______”). The right side is a dependent temporal clause (“as light intensity increases”) paired with an independent clause (“so does the rate...”) that elaborates the first statement.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for elaboration
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration or specification of what precedes it; B correctly uses the colon to signal that the “as... so...” construction specifies how light intensity affects the reaction rate.

The other choices either omit punctuation (A), misuse a connector (C), or use a comma (D) without clearly signaling elaboration.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
